subject: Accounting
title: Compound Journal Entries
description: A 60-second lesson on Compound Journal Entries.

hook.text: A customer pays $500 in cash and $500 by credit card for a $1,000 purchase. That's three accounts in one entry.

buildup.text: Most entries have one debit and one credit. Compound entries involve two or more of each.

discovery.text: The rule still applies: total debits must equal total credits, no matter how many accounts are involved.

twist.text: Complex transactions like payroll can touch a dozen accounts in a single compound entry.

climax.text: Compound entries keep the books clean—one entry captures the full economic reality of the transaction.

punchline.text: More accounts, same rule: debits must equal credits.

quiz.question: What makes a journal entry compound?
options: It involves more than two accounts
